# 0-31 Saichi Maru (Annette)

*Security Walker Robot*

**Designation:** 0-31 Saichi Maru | **Type:** Size 5 walker robot | **TL:** 12+ | **Status:** Active | **Owner:** Annette

![0-31 Saichi Maru portrait](/wiki/media/Saichi-Maru-HEAD.png)

| Field | Value |
| ----- | ----- |
| Designation | 0-31 Saichi Maru |
| Nickname | Saichi |
| Type | Security Walker Robot |
| Tech Level | TL12+ |
| Brain Type | Advanced TL12+ |
| Speed | 21m |
| Hits | 20 |
| Protection | 4 / 10 vs laser |
| Cost | Cr290,900 |

## Overview

0-31 Saichi Maru is a size 5 walker robot configured for gunner and security duties. Known by the crew as Saichi, the unit combines a hardened security chassis, an Advanced TL12+ brain, integrated sensors, and mounted laser weapons into a compact patrol and shipboard defense platform.

The robot is operated by Annette and is built around battlefield awareness, corridor control, and reliable response under pressure rather than social nuance or independent improvisation.

title: Alain Mercier
text:
# Alain Mercier

## Basic Information

**Full Name:** Alain Étienne Mercier
**Species:** Human (Solomani)
**Citizenship:** Solomani Confederation (Jardin)
**Birth Year:** 1061
**Birthplace:** Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin
**Occupation:** Minister of Genetics, Government of Jardin
**Political Affiliation:** Solomani Party (Technocratic Reform Bloc)
**Status:** Deceased (1105)

---

## Overview

Alain Mercier was a Jardinese geneticist, civil servant, and politician who served as Minister of Genetics during the administration of Supreme President Leo Duprey. Widely regarded as one of the foremost experts on population genetics and demographic analysis within the Solomani Confederation, Mercier oversaw the Ministry of Genetics during a period of unprecedented expansion in genetic research and data collection.

Although generally viewed as a loyal supporter of the Solomani Cause, Mercier developed a reputation for placing scientific findings above political convenience. His insistence on evidence-based policy earned him respect among researchers and academics but often placed him at odds with powerful political interests.

Mercier became a figure of subsector-wide interest shortly before his death when he announced plans to release the results of a major long-term demographic study at the upcoming Solomani Party Congress.

---

## Early Life

Born in the capital city of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Mercier was the son of two senior administrators employed by Jardin's Eugenics Bureau. Identified at an early age as possessing exceptional intellectual aptitude, he was selected for advanced educational programs and graduated with distinction from the Jardin Institute of Biological Sciences.

His academic work focused on long-term population stability, hereditary trait distribution, and the interaction between social planning and genetic outcomes. Several of his early papers became required reading within Confederation genetics programs.

---

## Academic Career

Before entering government service, Mercier spent nearly two decades as a researcher and lecturer. He gained prominence through his work analyzing the effects of large-scale demographic interventions across multiple generations.

His findings challenged several accepted assumptions regarding population management and brought him to the attention of senior Party officials. Unlike many researchers who remained within academia, Mercier demonstrated an unusual ability to communicate complex scientific findings to political leaders.

By his mid-forties he had become one of the Confederation's most influential voices on genetics policy.

---

## Minister of Genetics

Mercier was appointed Minister of Genetics in 1097 following the retirement of his predecessor.

During his tenure the Ministry expanded several major initiatives, including:

* Confederation-wide genetic archive modernization.
* Longitudinal population studies.
* Enhanced hereditary disease screening.
* Interplanetary demographic monitoring.
* Research into population resilience and long-term genetic stability.

Although publicly supportive of existing policy, Mercier repeatedly argued that no social system could remain effective unless it was subjected to continual scientific review.

His ministry became known for its unusually rigorous standards and independence from factional politics.

---

## Political Views

Mercier was often described as a technocrat rather than an ideologue.

While loyal to Jardin and the Solomani Cause, he frequently emphasized that scientific institutions should not be influenced by political considerations.

His most famous public statement was delivered shortly before his death:

> "We owe it to the citizens of Jardin to follow the data wherever it leads. The strength of our society has always been built upon scientific rigor, honest evaluation, and the courage to improve."

Supporters viewed this philosophy as evidence of intellectual integrity.

Critics argued that it demonstrated political naivety.

---

## Final Months

In the final year of his life, Mercier directed a comprehensive review of demographic and genetic data collected over multiple generations.

At a press conference held at the Hall of Genetics, he announced that his ministry would reveal significant findings at the upcoming Solomani Party Congress.

The announcement generated intense speculation throughout political, military, and academic circles.

Observers noted that Mercier's remarks were unusually direct and appeared to suggest conclusions with potentially far-reaching implications.

---

## Death

On 143-1105, while attending official business at the Hall of Genetics in Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Mercier was assassinated.

The murder shocked Jardin and rapidly became one of the most significant criminal and political investigations in recent Confederation history.

His death occurred only weeks before the scheduled release of his ministry's report.

The contents of the report have never been publicly disclosed.

---

## Legacy

Alain Mercier remains a controversial figure in Jardinese history.

To supporters, he embodied the principle that scientific truth should guide public policy regardless of political consequences.

To critics, he represented a dangerous willingness to question institutions that had maintained stability for centuries.

His unfinished work continues to be the subject of speculation among historians, political analysts, and genetic researchers throughout the Solomani Confederation.

title: Arcosphere Rousseau
text:
# Arcosphère Rousseau

## Rousseau Arcology

**Location:** Central Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Urban Arcology, Residential Megastructure, Commercial Hub

**Administrative Authority:** Arcology Administration Directorate

**Governor:** Adrien Pelletier

**Population:** 1.8 Billion Permanent Residents

**Daily Population:** Approximately 2.3 Billion

**Public Access:** Open

**Nickname:** *"Rousseau"*

---

# Overview

The **Arcosphère Rousseau** (Rousseau Arcology) is the largest structure on Jardin and one of the most densely populated urban environments in the Solomani Confederation.

Dominating the center of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, the arcology forms a massive crescent surrounding the Grand Axis and serves as the residential, commercial, industrial, and cultural heart of the planetary capital.

To outsiders, Rousseau appears impossible.

To residents, it is simply home.

Nearly one in seven citizens of Jardin lives within its walls.

Entire generations are born, educated, employed, married, and buried without ever leaving the arcology.

Government planners proudly describe Rousseau as:

> "Human civilization perfected."

Residents typically describe it as:

> "Crowded."

---

# History

## The Arcology Initiative

Construction began nearly two centuries ago during the Great Expansion.

Rapid population growth threatened to consume valuable agricultural land surrounding Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

Rather than expand outward indefinitely, planners chose a different solution.

The city would expand upward.

The Arcology Initiative combined:

* Housing
* Industry
* Transportation
* Commerce
* Education
* Recreation

into a single integrated structure.

The project became one of the largest construction efforts in Confederation history.

---

## The Rousseau Doctrine

Named after the ancient Terran philosopher Jean-Jacques Rousseau, the arcology was intended to demonstrate that careful planning could create a superior urban environment.

Government publications described it as:

> "A city designed for citizens rather than automobiles."

The original plans envisioned housing approximately 800 million residents.

Reality proved more complicated.

---

## The Quiet Expansion

Over successive generations, unused maintenance spaces, storage sections, and service corridors were gradually converted into residential and commercial districts.

Some expansions were legal.

Others were not.

The result is a structure far larger than official plans ever intended.

No one knows its true size.

---

# Architecture

From above, Rousseau resembles an immense crescent embracing the Grand Axis of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

The structure rises hundreds of meters above the city and extends deep beneath ground level.

Major features include:

* Residential towers
* Commercial districts
* Internal parks
* Transit networks
* Schools
* Hospitals
* Manufacturing centers

The arcology contains enough internal infrastructure to function independently for months if isolated.

---

# Major Districts

## Les Sommets

### The Summit Levels

The highest and most prestigious districts.

Home to:

* Senior Party officials
* Corporate executives
* Leading scientists
* Wealthy professionals

Features include:

* Private sky gardens
* Luxury apartments
* Exclusive clubs
* Fine dining establishments

Many apartments possess direct views of the Palace of Continuity.

Residents insist this is merely coincidence.

---

## Les Niveaux Civiques

### Civic Levels

The professional middle class.

Home to:

* Teachers
* Engineers
* Doctors
* Administrators
* Researchers

These districts represent the idealized vision of Jardin society.

Clean.

Efficient.

Safe.

Almost every government promotional video is filmed here.

---

## Les Communes

### Commons Levels

The largest section of Rousseau.

Home to more than a billion residents.

Contains:

* Housing blocks
* Schools
* Markets
* Restaurants
* Recreation facilities

This is where most ordinary citizens live.

Visitors often compare it to a city existing entirely indoors.

---

## Les Ateliers

### Industrial Levels

The manufacturing heart of the arcology.

Contains:

* Factories
* Warehouses
* Robot maintenance facilities
* Recycling systems
* Cargo handling centers

Thousands of freight vehicles move through the district every hour.

Most visitors never see it.

Most residents depend upon it.

---

## Les Profondeurs

### The Depths

The lowest officially recognized districts.

Contains:

* Utility systems
* Transit infrastructure
* Water processing facilities
* Power distribution networks

Officially sparsely populated.

Unofficially not.

---

# The Unofficial City

## Les Ombres

### The Shadows

The name given to unregistered districts hidden within the arcology.

These areas do not officially exist.

Generations of unauthorized construction have created:

* Informal neighborhoods
* Hidden marketplaces
* Illegal workshops
* Refugee communities
* Smuggling routes

Authorities periodically deny their existence.

Everyone else knows better.

---

# Daily Life

Life inside Rousseau rarely requires leaving the structure.

Residents have access to:

* Employment
* Education
* Entertainment
* Healthcare
* Transportation

within walking distance.

Many citizens spend months without seeing open sky.

Artificial lighting systems simulate natural day-night cycles.

Most residents stop noticing.

---

# Transportation

Rousseau possesses one of the most advanced transit systems in the Confederation.

Transportation methods include:

* Maglev trains
* Moving pedestrian corridors
* Autonomous shuttles
* Freight elevators
* Grav lifts

The average resident can reach any major district within thirty minutes.

---

# Commerce

The arcology contains millions of businesses.

Everything from:

* Street vendors
* Family restaurants
* Boutique manufacturers
* Corporate headquarters

can be found within Rousseau.

Many of the Confederation's largest corporations maintain offices here.

---

# Culture

Rousseau possesses its own distinct identity.

Residents often describe themselves as:

> "Rousseans first, Jardinais second."

Whether this is intended as a joke remains unclear.

The arcology has developed:

* Unique slang
* Local traditions
* Distinct cuisine
* Neighborhood rivalries

Many citizens never fully identify with the broader city.

---

# Major Landmarks

## Forum Rousseau

The central public square.

Location of:

* Public speeches
* Demonstrations
* Cultural festivals

The largest indoor gathering space on Jardin.

---

## Market of a Thousand Worlds

A sprawling commercial district.

Contains goods from across the Confederation and beyond.

One of the best places on Jardin to find something unusual.

---

## The People's Conservatory

A vast indoor park.

Contains:

* Lakes
* Forests
* Walking paths

Many residents visit simply to experience something resembling nature.

---

## The Arcology Museum

Documents the history of Rousseau and urban development throughout the Confederation.

Popular with school groups.

Not popular with schoolchildren.

---

# Security

Public security is maintained by:

* Arcology Security Directorate
* Municipal Police
* SolSec liaison officers

Security levels vary considerably between districts.

The Summit Levels are among the safest places on Jardin.

The Shadows are not.

---

# Criminal Activity

Despite government claims, Rousseau possesses a substantial underworld.

Activities include:

* Smuggling
* Identity fraud
* Unregistered robotics
* Counterfeiting
* Information theft

Most criminal activity occurs within The Shadows.

---

# The Jardin Syndicate

The arcology serves as the primary operational center of the Jardin Syndicate.

Officially this is false.

Everyone knows it is true.

Numerous front organizations operate within the Commons and Industrial districts.

Their influence is particularly strong among:

* Migrant populations
* Refugee communities
* Unregistered residents

The Syndicate's unofficial slogan is frequently heard in the Shadows:

> "Everything is possible. It simply isn't approved."

---

# Notable Individuals

## Adrien Pelletier

**Governor of Rousseau**

A dedicated administrator who genuinely believes the arcology represents humanity's future.

---

## Nadia Giroux

**Investigative Journalist**

Known for exposing corruption and embarrassing powerful individuals.

Frequently receives anonymous threats.

Frequently ignores them.

---

## Sister Celeste Moreau

**Community Organizer**

Operates charitable programs throughout the lower districts.

Widely respected by residents.

---

## Jacques Martin

**Business Executive**

Officially owns several logistics companies.

Unofficially rumored to maintain extensive connections within the Jardin Syndicate.

---

# Public Opinion

Government officials point to Rousseau as proof of the success of Solomani planning.

Residents generally agree.

They simply disagree about why.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Levels

Entire districts sealed during construction still exist beneath the arcology.

---

## The Hidden Registry

An illegal copy of citizen records exists somewhere within The Shadows.

---

## The Shepherd's Court

Marcel Delphine allegedly maintains a hidden residence somewhere inside Rousseau.

No one agrees where.

---

## The Ghost Transit Line

A maglev route appears on no official maps but continues to operate.

Passengers report reaching destinations that should not exist.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Citizen

A resident vanishes from every government database.

The only lead points toward the Shadows.

### Arcology Election

A local Party Congress threatens to expose major corruption.

Several factions seek outside assistance.

### Ghost District

Maintenance crews discover a forgotten neighborhood sealed for decades.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

The Travellers receive an invitation to meet Marcel Delphine.

The location changes every time it is transmitted.

### The Registry War

The Hall of Chosen Futures and the Jardin Syndicate begin a quiet conflict over stolen citizen records.

The Travellers become caught in the middle.

### Lost Beneath Rousseau

A child disappears into the unregistered sections of the arcology.

Finding them requires entering places that officially do not exist.

---
:::gm

# Referee Notes

Rousseau should feel alive.

Unlike the Palace of Continuity, which represents authority, or the Hall of Chosen Futures, which represents administration, Rousseau represents people.

It contains everything.

Success and failure.

Wealth and poverty.

Order and chaos.

Idealism and corruption.

If the Palace is the mind of Jardin and the Hall is its nervous system, then Rousseau is its beating heart.

Most campaigns set on Jardin will eventually spend more time here than anywhere else.

The arcology is large enough to contain an entire campaign by itself.


:::gm
GM-only truth goes here.
:::

The average resident can reach any major district within thirty minutes.

---

# Commerce

The arcology contains millions of businesses.

Everything from:

* Street vendors
* Family restaurants
* Boutique manufacturers
* Corporate headquarters

can be found within Rousseau.

Many of the Confederation's largest corporations maintain offices here.

---

# Culture

Rousseau possesses its own distinct identity.

Residents often describe themselves as:

> "Rousseans first, Jardinais second."

Whether this is intended as a joke remains unclear.

The arcology has developed:

* Unique slang
* Local traditions
* Distinct cuisine
* Neighborhood rivalries

Many citizens never fully identify with the broader city.

---

# Major Landmarks

## Forum Rousseau

The central public square.

Location of:

* Public speeches
* Demonstrations
* Cultural festivals

The largest indoor gathering space on Jardin.

---

## Market of a Thousand Worlds

A sprawling commercial district.

Contains goods from across the Confederation and beyond.

One of the best places on Jardin to find something unusual.

---

## The People's Conservatory

A vast indoor park.

Contains:

* Lakes
* Forests
* Walking paths

Many residents visit simply to experience something resembling nature.

---

## The Arcology Museum

Documents the history of Rousseau and urban development throughout the Confederation.

Popular with school groups.

Not popular with schoolchildren.

---

# Security

Public security is maintained by:

* Arcology Security Directorate
* Municipal Police
* SolSec liaison officers

Security levels vary considerably between districts.

The Summit Levels are among the safest places on Jardin.

The Shadows are not.

---

# Criminal Activity

Despite government claims, Rousseau possesses a substantial underworld.

Activities include:

* Smuggling
* Identity fraud
* Unregistered robotics
* Counterfeiting
* Information theft

Most criminal activity occurs within The Shadows.

---

# The Jardin Syndicate

The arcology serves as the primary operational center of the Jardin Syndicate.

Officially this is false.

Everyone knows it is true.

Numerous front organizations operate within the Commons and Industrial districts.

Their influence is particularly strong among:

* Migrant populations
* Refugee communities
* Unregistered residents

The Syndicate's unofficial slogan is frequently heard in the Shadows:

> "Everything is possible. It simply isn't approved."

---

# Notable Individuals

## Adrien Pelletier

**Governor of Rousseau**

A dedicated administrator who genuinely believes the arcology represents humanity's future.

---

## Nadia Giroux

**Investigative Journalist**

Known for exposing corruption and embarrassing powerful individuals.

Frequently receives anonymous threats.

Frequently ignores them.

---

## Sister Celeste Moreau

**Community Organizer**

Operates charitable programs throughout the lower districts.

Widely respected by residents.

---

## Jacques Martin

**Business Executive**

Officially owns several logistics companies.

Unofficially rumored to maintain extensive connections within the Jardin Syndicate.

---

# Public Opinion

Government officials point to Rousseau as proof of the success of Solomani planning.

Residents generally agree.

They simply disagree about why.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Levels

Entire districts sealed during construction still exist beneath the arcology.

---

## The Hidden Registry

An illegal copy of citizen records exists somewhere within The Shadows.

---

## The Shepherd's Court

Marcel Delphine allegedly maintains a hidden residence somewhere inside Rousseau.

No one agrees where.

---

## The Ghost Transit Line

A maglev route appears on no official maps but continues to operate.

Passengers report reaching destinations that should not exist.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Citizen

A resident vanishes from every government database.

The only lead points toward the Shadows.

### Arcology Election

A local Party Congress threatens to expose major corruption.

Several factions seek outside assistance.

### Ghost District

Maintenance crews discover a forgotten neighborhood sealed for decades.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

The Travellers receive an invitation to meet Marcel Delphine.

The location changes every time it is transmitted.

### The Registry War

The Hall of Chosen Futures and the Jardin Syndicate begin a quiet conflict over stolen citizen records.

The Travellers become caught in the middle.

### Lost Beneath Rousseau

A child disappears into the unregistered sections of the arcology.

Finding them requires entering places that officially do not exist.

---
gm
GM-only truth goes here.
:::

title: Crèche District 14
text:
# Crèche District 14

*"Every Child, A Future."*

**Location:** Eastern Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Type:** State Child Development Arcology

**Administrative Authority:** Directorate of Child Development

**Status:** Active

**Population:** 1.2 Million Children

**Staff:** 180,000 Educators, Medical Personnel, and Administrators

**Nickname:** District 14

---

# Overview

Crèche District 14 is one of the largest child development complexes on Jardin.

It serves as home, school, community, and developmental center for over a million children from infancy through adolescence.

Unlike most societies, children in District 14 are not raised by biological parents.

They are raised by the State.

To most Jardinese citizens, this is entirely normal.

To foreign visitors, it is often deeply unsettling.

---

# First Impressions

Visitors expecting a grim institution are often surprised.

District 14 is beautiful.

Clean.

Quiet.

Orderly.

Safe.

Children play in parks.

Students attend classes.

Sports teams practice in open fields.

Music drifts from performance halls.

Everything appears healthy and happy.

That is what makes visitors uncomfortable.

Nothing appears wrong.

---

# Philosophy

District 14 operates under a simple belief:

> No child should be limited by the circumstances of birth.

Every child receives:

- Housing
- Nutrition
- Medical care
- Education
- Psychological support
- Recreational opportunities

The system does not view itself as replacing parents.

It views itself as replacing inequality.

---

# Structure

Children are organized into developmental cohorts called:

## Maisons

Houses.

Each Maison contains approximately:

- 120 Children
- 12 Mentors
- 8 Educational Staff
- Medical Support Personnel

Children remain with the same Maison for years.

The House becomes their family.

---

# Mentors

The closest equivalent to parents.

Mentors are highly trained professionals responsible for:

- Emotional development
- Discipline
- Guidance
- Education support

Many remain with the same cohort for decades.

Former students frequently maintain lifelong relationships with mentors.

---

# Education

District 14 contains:

- Primary Schools
- Secondary Schools
- Technical Academies
- Arts Programs
- Athletic Institutes

Educational outcomes are among the highest in the Confederation.

The district produces:

- Scientists
- Administrators
- Military Officers
- Engineers
- Artists

at remarkable rates.

---

# Daily Life

Children live structured lives.

A typical day includes:

- Education
- Physical Exercise
- Community Service
- Social Development
- Recreation

Schedules are carefully balanced.

The goal is not obedience.

The goal is optimization.

---

# The Memory Gardens

The most unusual area of District 14.

Every child possesses a personal record archive.

Messages from biological parents.

Medical records.

Academic achievements.

Personal milestones.

Upon adulthood citizens gain access to these records.

For many, this is the first time they learn much about their biological origins.

---

# Relationship with Biological Parents

Biological parents are not secret.

Nor are they central.

Most children know:

- Their genetic contributors
- Their heritage
- Their family history

But these relationships are considered secondary.

The Maison is primary.

---

# Public Opinion

Most Jardinese consider District 14 a success.

Supporters point to:

- Low crime
- Excellent education
- Social equality
- High productivity

Critics argue that something important has been lost.

Most struggle to define exactly what.

---

# Security

District 14 maintains its own branch of Social Solidarity.

Officers are rarely seen.

Problems are usually addressed long before force becomes necessary.

Visitors often describe the district as:

> "The safest place on the planet."

---

# Notable Locations

## Hall of First Steps

Infant development center.

Every citizen of District 14 begins here.

---

## Unity Stadium

Largest youth athletics facility on Jardin.

---

## Garden of Futures

A massive public park where graduation ceremonies occur.

---

## The Reflection Pool

A quiet memorial dedicated to former students.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Director Elise Fournier

Administrator of District 14.

Widely respected.

Known throughout Jardin as:

> "The Mother of a Million."

---

## Mentor Jean Mercier

One of the oldest serving mentors.

Three generations of graduates have passed through his Maison.

---

## Dr. Amelie Rousseau

Chief Developmental Psychologist.

Her research shapes educational policy across Jardin.

---

# What Foreigners Notice

Visitors usually report three things:

1. The children appear remarkably confident.
2. The children appear unusually independent.
3. The children rarely speak about parents.

The third observation is usually the one that stays with them.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Maison

A House that supposedly disappeared from official records decades ago.

---

## The Original Children

Records of the first generation raised under the Perfection System remain sealed.

---

## The Questionnaires

Every child completes extensive annual assessments.

Conspiracy theorists claim these evaluations serve purposes beyond education.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

District 14 should never feel like an orphanage.

It should feel like a society that genuinely believes it has solved childhood.

Visitors should leave asking themselves:

> "The children seem happy."

And then immediately wondering:

> "So why does this place make me uncomfortable?"


GM-only truth goes here.
:::

title: Hall des Futurs Choisis
text:
# Hall des Futurs Choisis

## Hall of Chosen Futures

**Location:** Government District, Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Ministry Complex, Civic Registry, Genetic Administration Center

**Population:** Approximately 120,000 employees and visitors daily

**Administrative Authority:** Ministry of Genetics

**Current Minister:** Alain Mercier

**Nickname:** *"The Hall"*

---

# Overview

The **Hall des Futurs Choisis** (Hall of Chosen Futures) is among the most influential institutions on Jardin and arguably the most important government building outside the Palace of Continuity itself.

Officially, the Hall exists to coordinate the scientific, educational, demographic, and civic planning efforts that have guided Jardin's development for centuries.

In practice, the Hall serves as the point where government policy intersects with everyday life.

Every citizen on Jardin will interact with the Hall repeatedly throughout their lifetime.

Birth registrations, educational assessments, citizenship certifications, genetic consultations, robot registrations, family planning permits, immigration reviews, military aptitude evaluations, and countless other services are administered through its vast bureaucracy.

For many citizens, the Hall represents the triumph of science and reason over chaos.

For many foreigners, it represents something far more unsettling.

---

# Public Purpose

The Hall's official mission is inscribed above the main entrance:

> "The Future Is Not Found. It Is Built."

The institution exists to help citizens make informed decisions that contribute to both personal success and societal prosperity.

Government publications describe the Hall as:

* A center of scientific guidance
* A guardian of public welfare
* A steward of future generations
* A repository of civic records
* A facilitator of social opportunity

Critics throughout the Confederation often describe it as:

* A surveillance apparatus
* A demographic control center
* A social engineering institution

Most citizens reject such characterizations.

---

# History

## Foundation Era

The original Hall began as a simple census and population records office during Jardin's first century.

As population expanded, government planners discovered that accurate demographic forecasting provided significant economic advantages.

Successive administrations gradually expanded the office's authority.

What began as a registry evolved into a planning bureau.

The planning bureau evolved into a ministry.

The ministry eventually became the Hall.

---

## The Genetic Reforms

The modern Hall emerged during the Genetic Reforms of the sixth century.

Advances in biological sciences allowed officials to:

* Predict hereditary disorders
* Track population trends
* Improve public health outcomes
* Guide educational development

These programs proved remarkably successful.

Life expectancy increased.

Crime declined.

Educational outcomes improved.

The public generally supported further expansion.

---

## The Integration Period

During the following centuries dozens of independent agencies were merged into the Hall.

Functions absorbed included:

* Birth registration
* Citizenship management

The Hall des Futurs Choisis should not be portrayed as overtly sinister.

The institution is most effective when it appears competent, rational, and largely beneficial.

The discomfort comes not from oppression but from capability.

Everything works.

The records are accurate.

The recommendations are useful.

The staff are courteous.

The government genuinely improves many lives.

The question facing visitors is whether any institution should possess this much information, this much influence, and this much confidence in its ability to shape the future.

That question lies at the heart of modern Jardin.

GM-only truth goes here.
:::

---

# Overview

The **Hall des Futurs Choisis** (Hall of Chosen Futures) is among the most influential institutions on Jardin and arguably the most important government building outside the Palace of Continuity itself.

Officially, the Hall exists to coordinate the scientific, educational, demographic, and civic planning efforts that have guided Jardin's development for centuries.

In practice, the Hall serves as the point where government policy intersects with everyday life.

Every citizen on Jardin will interact with the Hall repeatedly throughout their lifetime.

Birth registrations, educational assessments, citizenship certifications, genetic consultations, robot registrations, family planning permits, immigration reviews, military aptitude evaluations, and countless other services are administered through its vast bureaucracy.

For many citizens, the Hall represents the triumph of science and reason over chaos.

For many foreigners, it represents something far more unsettling.

---

# Public Purpose

The Hall's official mission is inscribed above the main entrance:

> "The Future Is Not Found. It Is Built."

The institution exists to help citizens make informed decisions that contribute to both personal success and societal prosperity.

Government publications describe the Hall as:

* A center of scientific guidance
* A guardian of public welfare
* A steward of future generations
* A repository of civic records
* A facilitator of social opportunity

Critics throughout the Confederation often describe it as:

* A surveillance apparatus
* A demographic control center
* A social engineering institution

Most citizens reject such characterizations.

---

# History

## Foundation Era

The original Hall began as a simple census and population records office during Jardin's first century.

As population expanded, government planners discovered that accurate demographic forecasting provided significant economic advantages.

Successive administrations gradually expanded the office's authority.

What began as a registry evolved into a planning bureau.

The planning bureau evolved into a ministry.

The ministry eventually became the Hall.

---

## The Genetic Reforms

The modern Hall emerged during the Genetic Reforms of the sixth century.

Advances in biological sciences allowed officials to:

* Predict hereditary disorders
* Track population trends
* Improve public health outcomes
* Guide educational development

These programs proved remarkably successful.

Life expectancy increased.

Crime declined.

Educational outcomes improved.

The public generally supported further expansion.

---

Functions absorbed included:

* Birth registration
* Citizenship management
* Immigration processing
* Marriage licensing
* Educational placement
* Robot registration
* Population statistics
* Genetic counseling

title: Jardin Syndicate
summary: The **Jardin Syndicate** is one of the largest and most influential criminal organizations operating within the Jardin Subsector. Originally founded on Jardin itself, the Syndicate has expanded across dozens of worlds, establishing networks that span both Confederation and Imperial territory.
text:
# Syndicat Jardin

## Jardin Syndicate

**Type:** Interstellar Criminal Organization

**Area of Operations:** Jardin Subsector and neighboring regions on both sides of the Solomani-Imperial border

**Primary Activities:**

* Human trafficking
* Refugee exploitation
* Identity fraud
* Smuggling
* Money laundering
* Counterfeiting
* Illegal migration
* Labor racketeering

**Threat Assessment:** High

**Status:** Active

**Known Leader:** [[Marcel Delphine]] ("The Shepherd")

**Headquarters:** Unknown

**Suspected Primary Operations Center:** Rousseau Arcology, Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

---

# Overview

The **Jardin Syndicate** is one of the largest and most influential criminal organizations operating within the Jardin Subsector. Originally founded on Jardin itself, the Syndicate has expanded across dozens of worlds, establishing networks that span both Confederation and Imperial territory.

Unlike traditional criminal organizations, the Syndicate built its power not through narcotics, gambling, or protection rackets, but through control of migration.

The organization specializes in exploiting populations trapped between political systems.

Every year thousands of individuals seek escape from persecution, discrimination, economic hardship, or political repression. The Syndicate positions itself as a solution.

To refugees, they offer hope.

To governments, they provide plausible deniability.

To criminals, they provide opportunity.

To their victims, they are predators.

Their unofficial motto is said to be:

> "Everyone deserves a future."

The organization never specifies whose future profits from the arrangement.

---

# Origins

## The Border Opportunity

The Syndicate emerged approximately sixty years ago during a period of increasing migration between hard-line Solomani worlds and neighboring Imperial systems.

Jardin occupied a unique position.

As a prosperous world situated near the border, it served as both a destination and transit hub for displaced populations.

Certain groups found themselves particularly vulnerable:

* Non-Solomani humans
* Mixed-race populations
* Political dissidents
* Religious minorities
* Unregistered migrants
* Stateless individuals

A market emerged.

Someone was always willing to pay for transport.

Someone was always willing to sell it.

---

## The First Networks

Initially the organization consisted of independent brokers and shipping agents.

Most operated legally.

Some did not.

Over time these networks merged.

A loose confederation of smugglers, recruiters, corrupt officials, and transport operators gradually evolved into a sophisticated criminal enterprise.

The name "Jardin Syndicate" first appeared in SolSec intelligence reports nearly fifty years ago.

---

# Structure

The Syndicate operates as a decentralized network rather than a traditional hierarchy.

Cells maintain substantial autonomy.

Many members never meet senior leadership.

This structure has proven highly resistant to law-enforcement efforts.

---

## Directorate

The inner leadership circle.

Responsible for:

* Strategic planning
* Financial operations
* Political relationships
* Major smuggling routes

Few individuals know the identities of all Directorate members.

---

## Placement Agencies

The public face of the organization.

These agencies advertise:

* Employment opportunities
* Immigration assistance
* Relocation services
* Refugee support

Many are entirely legitimate.

Some are not.

Citizens rarely know the difference.

---

## Transit Networks

Responsible for moving people across interstellar borders.

Methods include:

* Commercial passenger liners
* Low berths
* Freight shipments
* False identities
* Bribed port officials

The network extends throughout the subsector.

---

## Registry Specialists

Among the Syndicate's most valuable assets.

These specialists create:

* False identities
* Forged permits
* Altered citizenship records
* Fabricated employment histories

A skilled Registry Specialist can make a person appear to have existed for decades.

---

# Business Operations

## Refugee Transport

The Syndicate's most profitable enterprise.

Clients pay for:

* Transportation
* Documentation
* Employment placement
* Housing assistance

Some arrive safely.

Others do not.

---

## Identity Laundering

The process of constructing entirely new legal identities.

Packages may include:

* Birth certificates
* Citizenship records
* Educational histories
* Employment records

Prices vary dramatically.

---

## Debt Contracts

Many migrants lack sufficient funds.

The Syndicate offers financing.

Repayment terms are often severe.

Debtors may spend years working to satisfy obligations.

---

## Labor Placement

The Syndicate maintains relationships with employers throughout the subsector.

Some placements are legitimate.

Others resemble indentured servitude.

The distinction often depends on who is asked.

---

## Counterfeiting

The organization manufactures:

* Currency
* Identification documents
* Shipping manifests
* Government certifications

High-quality counterfeit records are among its most valuable products.

---

# Political Relationships

The Syndicate survives because powerful people occasionally benefit from its activities.

---

## Corrupt Officials

Some local politicians quietly use Syndicate services.

Benefits include:

* Removal of troublesome populations
* Reduction in social tensions
* Access to unofficial labor pools

---

## Business Interests

Several corporations rely upon labor supplied through Syndicate channels.

Publicly they deny involvement.

---

## SolSec

The relationship between SolSec and the Syndicate remains complex.

Officially:

> The Syndicate is a criminal enterprise and enemy of the Confederation.

Unofficially:

Individual operations have occasionally intersected with broader political objectives.

Rumors persist that some Syndicate leaders possess information capable of embarrassing senior officials.

---

# Presence on Jardin

Jardin remains the organization's symbolic homeland.

The Syndicate's influence is strongest within:

* Rousseau Arcology
* Port Sainte-Jeanne
* Utility Canal districts
* Independent cargo facilities

Many legitimate businesses unknowingly interact with Syndicate-controlled intermediaries.

---

# Public Front Organizations

Known examples include:

### Nouvelle Horizons Placement Services

Employment and relocation agency.

### Future Path Migration Consulting

Immigration assistance organization.

### Citizens Aid Cooperative

Refugee support charity.

### Border Transit Logistics

Shipping and transportation firm.

Each has appeared in multiple investigations.

None have been successfully linked to senior Syndicate leadership.

---

# Common Sayings

### "Everyone deserves a future."

Often associated with the Syndicate.

### "The Hall gives you a future. The Syndicate lets you choose one."

Popular among anti-government circles.

### "The Shepherd knows a way."

A phrase heard among migrant communities.

---

# Rivals

## SolSec

The Syndicate's most dangerous enemy.

## Ministry of Justice

Responsible for many anti-Syndicate investigations.

## Independent Smugglers

Frequently absorbed or eliminated.

## Local Criminal Organizations

Often become subordinate partners.

---

# Rumors

## The Registry Mirror

The Syndicate allegedly possesses a complete copy of the Hall of Chosen Futures Citizen Registry.

Most experts consider this impossible.

---

## The Hidden Fleet

The Syndicate supposedly controls dozens of transport vessels operating under false registrations.

---

## The Ghost Citizens

Thousands of identities in the Confederation database may belong to people who never existed.

---

## The Shepherd's Archive

Marcel Delphine is rumored to possess decades of blackmail material concerning politicians, Party officials, corporate executives, and military officers.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Family

A refugee family vanishes after arriving on Jardin.

Their final known contact was a Syndicate recruiter.

### Ghost Identity

The Travellers discover that one of their own identities now belongs to someone else.

### SolSec Operation

Agents recruit the Travellers to infiltrate a Syndicate migration network.

### The Shepherd's Offer

Marcel Delphine personally approaches the Travellers with a proposal.

### False Refugees

A group of supposed refugees may actually be foreign intelligence operatives.

### The Registry Leak

Evidence suggests the Syndicate has gained access to highly classified citizen records.

### Debt Collection

A desperate migrant asks the Travellers for protection from Syndicate enforcers.

---


:::gm

# Referee Notes

The Jardin Syndicate should never be portrayed as purely evil or purely benevolent.

They help people.

They exploit people.

They save lives.

They destroy lives.

Many citizens owe their freedom to the Syndicate.

Many others lost everything because of it.

The organization's greatest strength is that it exists in the gray space between criminality, politics, humanitarianism, and commerce.

Every faction on Jardin has a reason to hate them.

Every faction on Jardin also has a reason to quietly tolerate them.


:::gm
Secret agenda, debts, and leverage.
:::

* Citizenship records
* Educational histories
* Employment records

Prices vary dramatically.

---

## Debt Contracts

Many migrants lack sufficient funds.

The Syndicate offers financing.

Repayment terms are often severe.

Debtors may spend years working to satisfy obligations.

---

## Labor Placement

The Syndicate maintains relationships with employers throughout the subsector.

Some placements are legitimate.

Others resemble indentured servitude.

The distinction often depends on who is asked.

---

## Counterfeiting

The organization manufactures:

* Currency
* Identification documents
* Shipping manifests
* Government certifications

High-quality counterfeit records are among its most valuable products.

---

# Political Relationships

The Syndicate survives because powerful people occasionally benefit from its activities.

---

## Corrupt Officials

Some local politicians quietly use Syndicate services.

Benefits include:

* Removal of troublesome populations
* Reduction in social tensions
* Access to unofficial labor pools

---

## Business Interests

Several corporations rely upon labor supplied through Syndicate channels.

Publicly they deny involvement.

---

## SolSec

The relationship between SolSec and the Syndicate remains complex.

Officially:

> The Syndicate is a criminal enterprise and enemy of the Confederation.

Unofficially:

Individual operations have occasionally intersected with broader political objectives.

Rumors persist that some Syndicate leaders possess information capable of embarrassing senior officials.

---

# Presence on Jardin

Jardin remains the organization's symbolic homeland.

The Syndicate's influence is strongest within:

* Rousseau Arcology
* Port Sainte-Jeanne
* Utility Canal districts
* Independent cargo facilities

Many legitimate businesses unknowingly interact with Syndicate-controlled intermediaries.

---

# Public Front Organizations

Known examples include:

### Nouvelle Horizons Placement Services

Employment and relocation agency.

### Future Path Migration Consulting

Immigration assistance organization.

### Citizens Aid Cooperative

Refugee support charity.

### Border Transit Logistics

Shipping and transportation firm.

Each has appeared in multiple investigations.

None have been successfully linked to senior Syndicate leadership.

---

# Common Sayings

### "Everyone deserves a future."

Often associated with the Syndicate.

### "The Hall gives you a future. The Syndicate lets you choose one."

Popular among anti-government circles.

### "The Shepherd knows a way."

A phrase heard among migrant communities.

---

# Rivals

## SolSec

The Syndicate's most dangerous enemy.

## Ministry of Justice

Responsible for many anti-Syndicate investigations.

## Independent Smugglers

Frequently absorbed or eliminated.

## Local Criminal Organizations

Often become subordinate partners.

---

# Rumors

## The Registry Mirror

The Syndicate allegedly possesses a complete copy of the Hall of Chosen Futures Citizen Registry.

Most experts consider this impossible.

---

## The Hidden Fleet

The Syndicate supposedly controls dozens of transport vessels operating under false registrations.

---

## The Ghost Citizens

Thousands of identities in the Confederation database may belong to people who never existed.

---

## The Shepherd's Archive

Marcel Delphine is rumored to possess decades of blackmail material concerning politicians, Party officials, corporate executives, and military officers.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Family

A refugee family vanishes after arriving on Jardin.

Their final known contact was a Syndicate recruiter.

### Ghost Identity

The Travellers discover that one of their own identities now belongs to someone else.

### SolSec Operation

Agents recruit the Travellers to infiltrate a Syndicate migration network.

### The Shepherd's Offer

Marcel Delphine personally approaches the Travellers with a proposal.

### False Refugees

A group of supposed refugees may actually be foreign intelligence operatives.

### The Registry Leak

Evidence suggests the Syndicate has gained access to highly classified citizen records.

### Debt Collection

A desperate migrant asks the Travellers for protection from Syndicate enforcers.

---


gm
Secret agenda, debts, and leverage.
:::

title: Le Palais de Continuité
text:
# Le Palais de Continuité

## The Palace of Continuity

**Location:** Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Seat of Government, Executive Complex, National Symbol

**Population:** Approximately 35,000 permanent staff

**Security Level:** Extreme

**Primary Authority:** Office of the Supreme Presidency

**Current Supreme President:** Leo Duprey

---

## Overview

The **Palais de Continuité** (Palace of Continuity) serves as both the administrative heart of Jardin and the symbolic embodiment of the world's political philosophy. Standing atop the heights overlooking central Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, the palace dominates the skyline and serves as the seat of the Supreme Presidency, the Supreme Directorate, and many of Jardin's most influential ministries.

To the citizens of Jardin, the palace represents stability, order, and the promise that each generation will improve upon the achievements of those that came before. Images of its distinctive towers appear on government broadcasts, educational materials, currency, and public monuments throughout the world.

To foreign observers, however, the palace often appears as something far more imposing: the command center of one of the most organized and closely managed societies in the Solomani Confederation.

---

## History

### Colonial Administration (Years 0-120)

The first government building on the site was little more than a fortified administrative center overlooking the original settlement of Sainte-Jeanne.

As the colony expanded, political authority became increasingly centralized. Successive governors added new offices, archives, and military command facilities.

### The Unification Crisis (Year 147)

Political divisions nearly fractured the young colony during the Unification Crisis. Several competing political movements sought control of the growing world.

The eventual victory of the Directorate resulted in the construction of a new governmental complex designed not merely to govern but to symbolize unity.

Construction of the original Palace of Continuity began shortly thereafter.

### The Century of Planning (Years 280-450)

As Jardin's population expanded into the billions, the palace became the center of increasingly sophisticated social planning efforts.

Entire ministries devoted to population management, education, genetics, and economic coordination established permanent headquarters within the complex.

Many of the structures visible today date from this period.

### Modern Era

Under the leadership of Supreme President Leo Duprey, extensive renovations modernized the palace's infrastructure and security systems.

Officially, these improvements were intended to improve administrative efficiency.

Unofficially, many believe the renovations included extensive underground command facilities capable of directing military and civil operations during planetary emergencies.

---

## Architecture

The palace complex occupies nearly six square kilometers of heavily developed government land.

The architecture reflects traditional Solomani monumental design principles:

* Massive scale
* Symmetry
* White stone facades
* Grand public spaces
* Elevated terraces
* Formal gardens
* Towering administrative spires

The most recognizable feature is the **Central Continuity Tower**, which rises nearly four hundred meters above the surrounding city.

At night, powerful illumination systems make the tower visible from nearly every district within Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

---

## Major Facilities

### Office of the Supreme Presidency

The official office and residence of the Supreme President.

Most executive decisions affecting Jardin ultimately pass through this office.

The Presidential Wing contains private meeting rooms, secure communications facilities, emergency command centers, and ceremonial halls.

---

### Chamber of Continuity

The most important political chamber on Jardin.

This vast circular hall hosts:

* Party Congress sessions
* National ceremonies
* State funerals
* Presidential inaugurations
* Major policy announcements

The walls contain the names of every Supreme President in the history of the world.

---

### Hall of Founders

A public museum dedicated to the early settlers of Jardin.

Schoolchildren regularly visit as part of their civic education.

The exhibits emphasize themes of sacrifice, scientific achievement, and collective responsibility.

---

### Directorate Wing

The meeting place of the Supreme Directorate.

This section contains offices for:

* National Security
* Economics
* Social Development
* Education
* Colonial Affairs
* Genetics
* Foreign Relations

Many of the most important decisions on Jardin are debated here before reaching the Presidency.

---

### Archives of Continuity

One of the largest government archives in the Confederation.

Contains:

* Historical records
* Census data
* Administrative archives
* Colonial documents
* Legislative records

Access is highly restricted.

Several sections remain permanently classified.

---

## Government Functions

The palace coordinates nearly every major function of planetary government.

These include:

### Executive Administration

Direct oversight of government agencies and ministries.

### Strategic Planning

Long-term economic, demographic, and military planning.

### Foreign Relations

Diplomatic coordination with neighboring systems and Confederation authorities.

### Emergency Management

Planetary crisis response and disaster planning.

### National Security

Coordination between military, intelligence, and internal security organizations.

---

## Current Leadership

### Supreme President Leo Duprey

The current ruler of Jardin.

A former Minister of Social Solidarity, Duprey rose through the Party apparatus over several decades.

Supporters credit him with:

* Economic expansion
* Political stability
* Increased military readiness
* Greater influence throughout the Pilgham region

Critics claim he has centralized too much authority within the Presidency.

Few make such criticisms publicly.

---

### Hélène Marchand

**Chief of Staff**

Responsible for managing the Presidency's daily operations.

Many observers believe she controls access to power more effectively than any cabinet minister.

---

### Alain Mercier

**Minister of Genetics**

One of the most influential figures in modern Jardin politics.

His upcoming presentation at the next Party Congress has become the subject of widespread speculation throughout government and academic circles.

Many believe he is positioning himself as a future successor to Duprey.

---

## Security

Security at the Palace of Continuity is among the most extensive on the planet.

Protective measures include:

* Presidential Guard regiments
* Internal Security Directorate personnel
* Automated surveillance networks
* Biometric access controls
* Electronic counterintelligence systems
* Dedicated anti-terror units

Visitors should assume they are under continuous observation.

Most are.

---

## Daily Life

Despite its immense importance, much of the palace operates like a small city.

Each day:

* Tens of thousands of civil servants report for work.
* Diplomatic delegations arrive from across the Confederation.
* Government committees meet continuously.
* School tours move through public sections.
* Journalists attend official briefings.
* Security personnel conduct routine patrols.

The majority of the palace is devoted to administration rather than politics.

---

## Cultural Significance

Few structures carry greater symbolic importance on Jardin.

Citizens commonly view the palace as proof that civilization can be directed through careful planning and competent governance.

The palace appears regularly in:

* Educational materials
* Recruitment campaigns
* Political broadcasts
* National celebrations
* Public art

For many citizens, the palace represents the very idea of Jardin itself.

---

## Common Sayings

### "All roads lead to Continuity."

Used when discussing problems that ultimately require government involvement.

### "The Palace remembers."

A reference to the government's extensive archives and long institutional memory.

### "Ask the Tower."

A sarcastic expression used when someone expects government intervention.

---

## Rumors and Secrets

Common rumors include:

* Secret genetic archives exist beneath the palace.
* A classified command bunker extends deep into the bedrock.
* Certain citizens have personal files far larger than normal.
* Succession planning for the Presidency has already begun.
* Hidden corridors connect the palace to military facilities elsewhere in the city.

Whether any of these stories are true remains unclear.

---

## Traveller Information

Visitors are generally permitted access to public portions of the complex.

Restricted areas require:

* Official sponsorship
* Government credentials
* Security clearance

Foreign Travellers often encounter additional scrutiny.

Imperial citizens in particular may find themselves subject to extensive questioning.

Weapons are prohibited throughout most of the complex.

Violations are dealt with swiftly.

---




:::gm
GM-only truth goes here.
## Adventure Hooks

1. A senior official disappears shortly before Party Congress.
2. Classified records vanish from the Archives of Continuity.
3. A foreign diplomat seeks discreet protection.
4. A whistleblower claims to possess evidence of a government conspiracy.
5. An assassination attempt threatens a major state ceremony.
6. A hidden chamber is uncovered during renovations.
7. Travellers are hired to recover stolen government data.
8. A presidential advisor secretly seeks passage off-world.
9. An Internal Security investigation targets the wrong suspect.
10. Evidence suggests someone has been altering historical records for decades.

---

## Referee Notes

The Palace of Continuity functions best as the political center of a campaign focused on intrigue, espionage, diplomacy, and conspiracy.

Nearly every major organization on Jardin has representatives operating within the palace, making it an ideal location to introduce plots that affect the entire world.

Many campaigns may begin here but discover that the true story extends far beyond its walls.
:::
